verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is secreted but its latent complex (LAP + mature TGF-β1 + LTBP) is covalently tethered to cell-surface presenting molecules — notably GARP (LRRC32) and LRRC33 on Tregs, platelets, and myeloid cells — via disulfide bonds between LAP cysteines and the TM partner. This produces a wash-resistant, surface-displayed latent TGF-β1 pool that is the target of clinical antibody programs (e.g., anti-GARP/TGF-β1 complex antibodies, latency-specific antibodies). The covalent disulfide tethering to a TM scaffold is the defining feature of stable_surface_attachment, distinct from reversible secreted equilibria.
